What went well: Used Tailwind CSS for premium, responsive landing pages.
What went poorly: Repeated HTML for Nav/Footers across 12+ pages.
What ideas do you have: Build a shared JS component loader for UI fragments.
How should we take action: Create components.js and move HTML to templates folder.
Guidelines: All future pages must use the shared component loader.

What went well: Implemented secure JWT login with role-based access control.
What went poorly: Users could occasionally access internal URLs without role check.
What ideas do you have: Build a server-side Role Guard for all API responses.
How should we take action: Update AuthGuard in auth_utils.py to strictly verify roles.
Guidelines: Never return sensitive data (e.g. passwords) in API responses.

What went well: Quick event approval flow for moderate/approving requests.
What went poorly: Lacked search/filter tools for managing hundreds of events.
What ideas do you have: Add real-time search and status filters to admin view.
How should we take action: Implement filters in admin.html and update backend query params.
Guidelines: Admins must have a full Audit Log of all approval actions.

What went well: Students can easily browse/filter upcoming free and paid events.
What went poorly: Students registered without completing profile details first.
What ideas do you have: Use a UI Overlay to block access until profile is 100% complete.
How should we take action: Add profile checks in student.js to force redirection if missing.
Guidelines: Mobile responsiveness is mandatory for the student dashboard.

What went well: Integrated familiar Google Sheets for participant management.
What went poorly: Difficulty tracking Hall availability across multiple dates.
What ideas do you have: Create a visual Hall status badge (Available/Occupied).
How should we take action: Build hall_availability API and display status badges in UI.
Guidelines: Every event must have a Hall; no events without venues.

What went well: Clean 14-table schema handling all core project entities.
What went poorly: Manual seeding of clubs/halls was slow and prone to duplicates.
What ideas do you have: Use ON CONFLICT handled SQL to make seeding safe to rerun.
How should we take action: Update database_creation.py with conflict handling logic.
Guidelines: Back up PostgreSQL before running destructive DROP commands.
